wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the SpareAutomation e-commerce platform. Explain the project's purpose as a Shopify-integrated automation equipment marketplace, its architecture overview, and key features including product management, cart functionality, quote generation, and trade account system. Document prerequisites (Node.js, Docker, Shopify API credentials), installation steps for both local development and containerized environments, environment configuration setup, and basic usage patterns. Include step-by-step instructions for setting up the development environment, running the application locally, and accessing the admin interface. Provide practical examples of common development tasks and troubleshooting tips for initial setup issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shopify API Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Shopify API configuration in SpareAutomation. Detail the setup process for connecting to Shopify stores, including environment variable configuration, API key management, and store domain setup. Document the Shopify client initialization, authentication methods, and connection pooling strategies. Include examples of configuring different Shopify environments (development, staging, production). Explain rate limiting configuration, retry mechanisms, and error handling patterns. Address security best practices for storing and managing Shopify credentials. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common connection issues and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the product management system. Explain the product data model, product listing and search functionality, product detail page implementation, and collection/category management. Document the Shopify API integration for product synchronization, inventory management, and pricing updates. Include configuration options for product display formats, filtering capabilities, and search algorithms. Provide practical examples demonstrating how to add new product types, customize product pages, implement advanced search filters, and handle product variants. Address common use cases like bulk product imports, image optimization, SEO optimization for products, and performance considerations for large catalogs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture & Foundation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the UI component architecture and foundation layer. Explain the shadcn/ui-based component system, design tokens, and architectural patterns used throughout SpareAutomation. Document the component composition principles, prop drilling strategies, and state management approaches within components. Include guidelines for creating new components following established patterns, accessibility standards implementation, and responsive design foundations. Provide examples of custom hooks usage, utility functions integration, and theme customization mechanisms. Address performance considerations, bundle optimization, and code splitting strategies for components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Route Configuration & Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for route configuration and structure in SpareAutomation. Explain the TanStack Router setup, root layout component architecture, and generated route tree management. Document the file-based routing conventions, nested route patterns, and dynamic route parameters. Include examples of creating new routes, handling route guards, and implementing protected routes with authentication. Detail the relationship between route files and page components, data loading strategies at the route level, and performance optimizations through lazy loading and code splitting. Address SEO considerations including meta tags, canonical URLs, and search engine optimization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Architecture & Core Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core form architecture and reusable UI components in SpareAutomation. Explain the React Hook Form integration pattern, form field composition, and component hierarchy. Document the base form components including Input, Textarea, Select, and Button with their props, styling options, and accessibility features. Include examples of form field registration, validation binding, and error display patterns. Document the form context provider, field state management, and performance optimization techniques. Provide guidelines for creating custom form components that integrate seamlessly with the existing form system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Global State Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for global state architecture in SpareAutomation. Explain the site configuration management system, root-level state initialization, and application-wide data sharing patterns. Document how site metadata, theme settings, and global preferences are managed across the application. Include examples of accessing global state from components, updating shared configuration, and handling state synchronization. Address state persistence strategies, environment-specific configurations, and state migration patterns. Provide guidelines for extending global state with new features while maintaining performance and memory efficiency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Containerization with Docker
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Docker containerization in SpareAutomation. Explain the multi-stage Docker build process, image optimization strategies, and container configuration. Document the docker-compose setup for local development and staging environments, including service orchestration, volume mounting, and environment variable management. Include concrete examples of building custom images, running containers locally, and managing container lifecycle. Address security best practices for container images, network configuration, and data persistence. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common containerization issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Client Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the API client architecture in SpareAutomation. Explain the overall design patterns used for API communication, including request/response handling, configuration management, and client initialization. Document the base client structure, interceptor patterns, and middleware implementation. Include examples of configuring different API endpoints, managing API keys and authentication tokens, and implementing custom request interceptors. Address connection pooling, timeout configurations, and retry mechanisms. Provide guidelines for extending the client with new API integrations and maintaining consistent error handling across all requests.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shopping Cart Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the shopping cart management system. Explain the implementation of adding products to cart, updating quantities, and removing items. Document the cart state management, including local storage persistence and session handling. Detail the integration with Shopify's cart API for real-time inventory and pricing. Include examples of cart UI components, event handling, and error states. Cover cart synchronization across devices, cart item validation, and stock availability checks. Address performance optimization for large carts and memory management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Data Model & Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for product entities and their relationships. Detail the Shopify product schema mapping to local TypeScript interfaces, including product variants, images, collections, pricing, and inventory fields. Document the data transformation layer that converts Shopify API responses into application-specific formats. Include field definitions, validation rules, and type constraints. Explain how product metadata, custom fields, and SEO information are handled. Provide examples of complete product objects with all nested properties and their data types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Flows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for authentication flows including user login, registration, and password recovery processes. Document the authentication state management, form validation, error handling, and user feedback mechanisms. Explain how authentication tokens are managed, session persistence, and security measures implemented. Include practical examples of extending authentication providers, implementing custom validation rules, and handling authentication errors. Address common authentication issues, debugging techniques, and best practices for secure user authentication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cart State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for cart state management. Explain the cart data structure, state persistence mechanisms, and how cart items are managed throughout the application lifecycle. Document the cart context or store implementation, including how cart state is synchronized across components and pages. Include examples of cart operations like adding items, updating quantities, removing items, and calculating totals. Address state synchronization between local storage and server-side cart data, handling cart conflicts, and implementing cart recovery after page refreshes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Data Model & Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the product data model and structure. Explain the product entity schema, field definitions, data types, and relationships with other entities like collections and variants. Document how product data is fetched from Shopify API, transformed, and cached. Include examples of product objects, variant structures, image handling, pricing models, and inventory tracking. Provide guidance on extending the data model for custom product attributes, implementing product metadata, and handling product lifecycle events.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the SpareAutomation system. Describe the high-level design using React-based frontend with Shopify integration, component-based architecture patterns, and service layer approach. Document the technology stack including React, TypeScript, Vite build system, and Docker containerization. Explain the directory structure organization, routing patterns, state management strategies, and data flow between components. Detail the integration architecture with Shopify Storefront API, authentication flows, and external service communications. Include system context diagrams showing component interactions, deployment topology, and infrastructure requirements. Address cross-cutting concerns like error handling, logging, SEO optimization, and performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Synchronization & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for product synchronization and management with Shopify. Thoroughly explain how products are fetched from Shopify Storefront API, cached locally, and rendered in the application. Document the product data model mapping between Shopify entities and local components. Include implementation details for product listing pages, product detail views, collection browsing, and search functionality. Cover real-time inventory updates, price synchronization, and variant management. Provide concrete examples of fetching product collections, handling product images, managing product variants, and implementing search filters. Address performance optimization techniques like pagination, lazy loading, and caching strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shopping Cart System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the shopping cart system. Thoroughly explain cart state management, add to cart functionality, cart persistence mechanisms, and cart item manipulation. Document the cart data structure, quantity management, price calculations, and tax handling. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how to customize cart behavior, implement cart validation rules, and integrate with external systems. Address common issues like cart synchronization across devices, inventory validation, and cart abandonment recovery. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for form-related UI components including Input, Textarea, Select, RadioGroup, Checkbox, Switch, and Form wrapper components. Document each component's props, validation integration, error handling, and styling customization options. Include practical examples of form building patterns, field validation with React Hook Form or similar libraries, and accessibility compliance. Provide guidance on form layout patterns, responsive behavior, and keyboard navigation support. Address common form scenarios like conditional fields, dynamic forms, and complex validation rules with concrete code examples from the actual codebase.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation Components & UI Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for navigation components and UI patterns in SpareAutomation. Thoroughly explain the implementation of the main navigation menu, breadcrumb system, header and footer navigation structures. Document component props, customization options, and responsive behavior patterns. Include concrete examples of adding new navigation items, implementing dropdown menus, and creating mobile-friendly navigation. Address accessibility compliance, keyboard navigation support, and screen reader compatibility. Explain state management for active routes, navigation highlighting, and user interaction patterns. Document integration with the routing system and client-side navigation handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation Strategies & Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for validation strategies and patterns used throughout SpareAutomation forms. Explain synchronous and asynchronous validation approaches, custom validator functions, and schema-based validation using Zod or similar libraries. Document field-level validation rules, cross-field validation, conditional validation logic, and real-time validation feedback. Include examples from support request and trade account forms showing email validation, phone number formatting, file size limits, and business rule enforcement. Address validation error messaging, localization support, and performance considerations for complex validation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Local Component State
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for local component state management patterns in SpareAutomation. Thoroughly explain React hooks usage including useState, useEffect, and custom hooks for component-specific state. Document form state management, UI interaction states, and temporary data handling within components. Include concrete examples of cart button interactions, product detail page state, and mobile responsiveness state. Address component state lifecycle, cleanup patterns, and performance optimization techniques. Explain when to use local vs global state, state lifting patterns, and component composition strategies for complex state scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: CI/CD Pipeline Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the CI/CD pipeline implementation using GitHub Actions. Explain the quality assurance workflow, automated testing integration, and code validation processes. Document the build pipeline stages, test execution, linting, and deployment triggers. Include examples of customizing the pipeline, adding new jobs, and configuring environment-specific deployments. Address error handling, notification systems, and pipeline monitoring. Provide guidelines for setting up local development workflows that mirror CI/CD processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shopify API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Shopify API integration in SpareAutomation. Document the Storefront API implementation, product data fetching, cart operations, and collection management. Explain GraphQL query structures, mutation patterns, and response handling. Include examples of product retrieval, inventory checking, cart manipulation, and checkout preparation. Document error handling specific to Shopify API responses, rate limiting considerations, and caching strategies for product data. Address authentication methods, store configuration, and environment setup. Provide troubleshooting guides for common Shopify API issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quote Generation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the quote generation system that converts cart items into professional quotes. Detail the quote creation workflow from cart data transformation to quote formatting. Explain the quote data structure, validation rules, and business logic. Document the integration between cart items and quote objects, including price calculations and product information mapping. Cover quote export capabilities, sharing mechanisms, and PDF generation if applicable. Include examples of quote customization, branding options, and email delivery integration. Address quote versioning, expiration handling, and conversion tracking to orders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Data Fetching & Caching
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for product data fetching and caching mechanisms. Thoroughly explain how products are retrieved from Shopify Storefront API, including GraphQL queries, error handling, and retry logic. Document the caching strategy implementation with local storage, memory caching, and cache invalidation policies. Cover pagination handling for large product catalogs, search query optimization, and real-time inventory updates. Include concrete examples of API calls, response processing, and cache management. Address performance considerations like request deduplication, background refresh, and offline support.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Dashboard & Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the user dashboard and profile management system. Explain account information display, profile editing capabilities, order history integration, and user preferences management. Document the data model for user profiles, API endpoints for profile operations, and real-time updates. Include examples of extending user profile fields, implementing role-based dashboard features, and integrating with external user management systems. Address user experience considerations, data synchronization, and performance optimization for profile operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cart Operations & Item Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for cart operations and item management functionality. Thoroughly explain how to add items to cart, update item quantities, remove items, and handle cart validation rules. Document the AddToCartButton component implementation, quantity adjustment logic, and price calculations including taxes and discounts. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how to implement custom cart operations, validate inventory availability, and handle error scenarios. Address cart item manipulation patterns, bulk operations, and integration with product detail pages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Browsing & Search
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for product browsing and search functionality. Document the collection page implementation, product grid layout, filtering mechanisms, and search algorithms. Explain pagination handling, sorting options, and performance optimization for large product catalogs. Include practical examples of implementing category navigation, advanced search filters, real-time search suggestions, and search result ranking. Address SEO considerations for product listings, lazy loading strategies, and mobile-responsive design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the core e-commerce features of SpareAutomation. Explain the product catalog system, shopping cart functionality, quote generation workflow, and user account management. Document the implementation details of product browsing, search capabilities, cart persistence, and quote creation from cart items. Include configuration options for product display, cart behavior customization, and quote export formats. Provide practical examples demonstrating how to extend product types, customize cart operations, and integrate additional payment methods. Address common use cases like bulk ordering, special pricing for trade accounts, and multi-currency support. Document the relationships between products, carts, quotes, and user sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cart & Order Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for cart and order processing integration with Shopify. Detail the implementation of shopping cart functionality, including adding items to cart, updating quantities, and removing products. Document the quote generation system that converts cart items into quotes for trade accounts. Explain the data flow between local cart state and Shopify's cart API. Include examples of handling cart persistence, managing cart sessions, and synchronizing cart data across devices. Cover order creation workflows, payment processing integration, and order status tracking. Address error handling for failed transactions, inventory conflicts, and network timeouts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quote Generation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the quote generation system. Explain the quote creation workflow from cart items, quote data structure, quote export and sharing capabilities, and quote management interface. Document the conversion process from cart to quote, quote formatting options, and integration with email services. Include practical examples demonstrating how to customize quote templates, implement quote approval workflows, and integrate with CRM systems. Address common use cases like bulk quoting, special pricing calculations, and quote versioning. Document the relationship between quotes and user accounts for trade account holders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout & Navigation Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for layout and navigation components including NavigationMenu, Breadcrumb, Sheet, Drawer, and Sidebar components. Document component props, event handlers, and customization options for creating responsive layouts and navigation patterns. Include examples of mobile-first navigation implementations, drawer behaviors, and breadcrumb trails. Provide guidance on accessibility considerations for navigation components, keyboard navigation support, and screen reader compatibility. Address performance optimization techniques for large navigation structures and lazy loading strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dynamic Routing & Parameters
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for dynamic routing and parameter handling in SpareAutomation. Explain the implementation of dynamic routes using path parameters, query string handling, and route validation. Document the product detail page routing pattern with handle-based URLs, error handling for invalid routes, and fallback mechanisms. Include examples of creating new dynamic routes, extracting and validating route parameters, and implementing route-specific data fetching. Address URL generation utilities, route linking patterns, and maintaining SEO-friendly URLs with dynamic content. Document error boundaries for route-level errors and loading states during data transitions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Forms Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for business-specific form implementations in SpareAutomation. Document the Support Request Form with its multi-step workflow, file attachment handling, and priority selection logic. Explain the Trade Account Application Form including document upload, company verification fields, and approval workflow integration. Include examples of form data transformation, API submission handling, success/error states, and user feedback mechanisms. Address form persistence, draft saving, and recovery functionality. Document integration with backend services, email notifications, and admin workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server State Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for server state handling and data synchronization in SpareAutomation. Explain client-server communication patterns, request/response state management, and data caching strategies. Document error handling for network failures, retry mechanisms, and optimistic updates. Include examples of API call state management, loading states, and error boundaries. Address state synchronization between multiple clients, real-time updates if applicable, and state hydration during server-side rendering. Provide guidelines for managing server state dependencies, implementing proper error recovery, and optimizing network requests for better user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web Server & Reverse Proxy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for web server configuration using nginx as a reverse proxy. Explain the nginx configuration file structure, routing rules, SSL/TLS setup, and performance optimizations. Document static asset serving, caching strategies, and security headers configuration. Include examples of load balancing setup, health checks, and monitoring integration. Address troubleshooting common nginx issues, log analysis, and performance tuning. Provide guidelines for development vs production configurations and environment-specific settings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Handling & Capture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for error handling and capture mechanisms in SpareAutomation. Explain the centralized error handling strategy, error categorization, and reporting workflows. Document the error capture system implementation, including stack trace collection, context gathering, and error serialization. Include examples of implementing custom error handlers, integrating with monitoring services, and creating user-friendly error pages. Address error logging strategies, debugging techniques, and production error monitoring. Document error recovery patterns, fallback mechanisms, and graceful degradation strategies. Provide guidelines for testing error scenarios and implementing proper error boundaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Order Workflow Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for order processing workflow integration with Shopify. Explain the complete order lifecycle from cart checkout to order confirmation. Document payment processing integration, including supported payment methods and security considerations. Detail order creation through Shopify's API, including order data mapping and fulfillment setup. Cover order status tracking, webhook handling for order updates, and error recovery mechanisms. Include examples of handling partial payments, refunds, and order modifications. Address order confirmation emails, inventory reservation, and shipping calculation integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Listing & Display Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for product listing and display components. Describe the ProductCard component architecture, including props interface, image handling, price display, and variant selection. Document the CollectionPage implementation with filtering, sorting, and pagination features. Explain responsive design patterns, accessibility considerations, and performance optimizations like lazy loading and virtual scrolling. Include usage examples with different product configurations, custom styling options, and integration patterns with other UI components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security & Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security documentation covering authentication security measures, session management, password policies, and user data protection. Document CSRF protection, XSS prevention, input sanitization, and secure communication protocols. Explain token storage strategies, session expiration handling, and logout procedures. Include guidelines for implementing multi-factor authentication, audit logging, and security monitoring. Address common security vulnerabilities, penetration testing considerations, and compliance requirements for user authentication systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cart Persistence & Synchronization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for cart persistence and synchronization mechanisms. Explain how cart data is stored locally using localStorage or cookies, and how it synchronizes with server-side cart data. Document the persistence strategy, including data serialization, conflict resolution, and offline cart handling. Include examples of implementing cart backup and restore functionality, handling cart migration between devices, and managing cart expiration policies. Address performance considerations for large carts and optimization techniques for cart loading and updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Detail Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for product detail page implementation. Explain the product information display, image galleries, variant selection, pricing display, and add-to-cart functionality. Document the dynamic routing system using product handles, SEO optimization techniques, and structured data markup. Include examples of customizing product layouts, implementing product recommendations, adding customer reviews, and integrating with the shopping cart system. Address performance optimization for product images, accessibility compliance, and cross-browser compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shopify Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Shopify integration in SpareAutomation. Thoroughly explain the Shopify Storefront API implementation, product synchronization mechanisms, inventory management, and order processing workflows. Document the configuration options for connecting to Shopify stores, managing product catalogs, handling real-time inventory updates, and processing orders. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how to fetch products, manage collections, handle cart operations, and process payments. Document error handling strategies, retry logic, rate limiting considerations, and debugging techniques. Address security considerations for API keys, data validation, and secure communication patterns. Explain the relationship between Shopify entities and local data models.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for payment integration within the Shopify ecosystem. Document how payment method marks are displayed and managed through the PaymentMarks component. Explain the integration with Shopify's payment gateways, including supported payment providers and their configuration. Detail the checkout flow integration, payment validation, and transaction handling. Include examples of customizing payment options, handling payment failures, and implementing custom payment flows. Address security considerations for payment data, PCI compliance requirements, and secure communication patterns. Document testing strategies for payment integrations and debugging payment-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Accounts & Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for the user accounts and authentication system. Explain user registration flow, login/logout mechanisms, account dashboard functionality, password recovery, and session management. Provide practical examples demonstrating how to extend user profiles, implement role-based access control, and integrate with external authentication services. Address common security considerations, user experience best practices, and troubleshooting authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Display Components

Address performance considerations for large datasets, virtual scrolling strategies, and memory optimization techniques for data-heavy components.

Explain state migration strategies, backup and restore functionality, and handling corrupted state data.
